diff --git a/app/api/routes/health.py b/app/api/routes/health.py
index a1b2c3d..e5f6g7h 100644
--- a/app/api/routes/health.py
+++ b/app/api/routes/health.py
@@ -7,12 +7,16 @@
 from __future__ import annotations
 
 import httpx
+import time
+from datetime import datetime
 
 from fastapi import APIRouter
 
 from app.core.config import get_settings
 from app.core.logging import get_logger
 from app.rag.vector_store import ChromaStore
+
+START_TIME = time.time()
 
 logger = get_logger(__name__)
 router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/health", tags=["health"])
@@ -47,11 +51,14 @@
         logger.warning("ChromaDB health check failed: %s", exc)
 
     status = "ok" if (ollama_ok and chroma_ok) else "degraded"
+    uptime_seconds = int(time.time() - START_TIME)
+    
     return {
         "status": status,
         "ollama": ollama_ok,
         "chromadb": chroma_ok,
         "llm_model": settings.OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL,
         "embed_model": settings.OLLAMA_EMBED_MODEL,
+        "uptime": f"{uptime_seconds}s",
+        "server_time": datetime.now().isoformat(),
     }

scenarios.title: Health check returns ok when all dependencies are healthy
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'ok' with ollama and chromadb flags true when both services are reachable.
preconditions: Ollama service is running and accessible at OLLAMA_BASE_URL
scenarios.expected_result: Response contains {status: 'ok', ollama: true, chromadb: true, llm_model: <configured_model>}

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded status when Ollama is unreachable
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with ollama=false when Ollama HTTP API is not reachable.
preconditions: Ollama service is stopped or OLLAMA_BASE_URL is misconfigured
scenarios.expected_result: Response contains {status: 'degraded', ollama: false, chromadb: true}

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded status when ChromaDB fails to initialize
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with chromadb=false when ChromaStore initialization or count() fails.
preconditions: ChromaDB is misconfigured or collection is corrupted
scenarios.expected_result: Response contains {status: 'degraded', ollama: true, chromadb: false}

scenarios.title: Health check handles Ollama connection timeout gracefully
scenarios.description: Verify that the 3-second timeout on Ollama HTTP client prevents hanging and returns degraded status.
preconditions: Ollama service is slow or unresponsive (simulated timeout scenario)
scenarios.expected_result: Request completes within 5 seconds total, returns {status: 'degraded', ollama: false} with warning logged

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded when all dependencies fail
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with both flags false when both Ollama and ChromaDB are unavailable.
scenarios.expected_result: Response contains {status: 'degraded', ollama: false, chromadb: false}

scenarios.title: Health check response includes correct llm_model from settings
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns the configured OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL value in the response.
preconditions: Application is configured with a specific OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL value
scenarios.expected_result: Response includes llm_model field matching the configured settings.OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL value

scenarios.title: Health check endpoint is accessible without authentication
scenarios.description: Verify that the health endpoint is intentionally public and does not require authentication headers.
scenarios.expected_result: Response returns 200 OK with health payload, not 401 Unauthorized

scenarios.title: Health check handles concurrent requests without race conditions
scenarios.description: Verify that multiple simultaneous requests to GET /api/health do not cause race conditions in ChromaStore initialization.
scenarios.expected_result: All 10 requests return 200 OK with consistent health status data

scenarios.title: Health check exception details are logged but not exposed in response
scenarios.description: Verify that when Ollama or ChromaDB throws exceptions, stack traces are logged internally but not returned in the API response.
preconditions: Ollama endpoint returns malformed response or connection error

steps.value: response does not contain stack trace or exception message

scenarios.expected_result: Response contains only {status, ollama, chromadb, llm_model} fields, no exception details exposed

generated_specs.content:
import os
import time
import pytest
import requests
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

BASE_URL = os.environ.get("BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000")


# SCENARIO_ID: 55c1bbf6-29bd-4769-b36a-b5cc9f4ca2c2
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.smoke
def test_health_check_returns_ok_when_all_dependencies_healthy(page: Page):
    """Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'ok' with ollama and chromadb flags true when both services are reachable."""
    page.goto(BASE_URL)
    response = page.context.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200
    data = response.json()
    
    assert data["status"] == "ok"
    assert data["ollama"] is True
    assert data["chromadb"] is True
    assert "llm_model" in data
    assert isinstance(data["llm_model"], str)
    assert len(data["llm_model"]) > 0


# SCENARIO_ID: ef0fd5e4-9e26-4bbe-91de-9407102bc388
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.resilience
def test_health_check_returns_degraded_when_ollama_unreachable(page: Page):
    """Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with ollama=false when Ollama HTTP API is not reachable."""
    page.goto(BASE_URL)
    response = page.context.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200
    data = response.json()
    
    # When Ollama is unreachable, expect degraded status with ollama=false
    # ChromaDB should still be healthy
    assert data["status"] == "degraded"
    assert data["ollama"] is False
    assert data["chromadb"] is True


# SCENARIO_ID: ebf0637c-59ac-4e16-b65e-fc201259e9f7
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.resilience
def test_health_check_returns_degraded_when_chromadb_fails(page: Page):
    """Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with chromadb=false when ChromaStore initialization fails."""
    page.goto(BASE_URL)
    response = page.context.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200
    data = response.json()
    
    # When ChromaDB fails, expect degraded status with chromadb=false
    # Ollama should still be healthy
    assert data["status"] == "degraded"
    assert data["ollama"] is True
    assert data["chromadb"] is False


# SCENARIO_ID: 994cd07b-2a3f-4757-a411-bfd5fc9272a3
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.timeout
def test_health_check_handles_ollama_connection_timeout_gracefully(page: Page):
    """Verify that the 3-second timeout on Ollama HTTP client prevents hanging and returns degraded status."""
    page.goto(BASE_URL)
    
    start_time = time.time()
    response = page.context.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/health")
    elapsed_time = time.time() - start_time
    
    # Request should complete within 5 seconds total (accounting for both Ollama timeout and ChromaDB check)
    assert elapsed_time < 5.0, f"Request took {elapsed_time}s, expected < 5s"
    
    assert response.status == 200
    data = response.json()
    
    # When Ollama times out, expect degraded status with ollama=false
    assert data["status"] == "degraded"
    assert data["ollama"] is False


# SCENARIO_ID: c749d5bb-aaf1-41af-96eb-d9d7cc4f214a
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.resilience
def test_health_check_returns_degraded_when_all_dependencies_fail(page: Page):
    """Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with both flags false when both Ollama and ChromaDB are unavailable."""
    page.goto(BASE_URL)
    response = page.context.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200
    data = response.json()
    
    # When all dependencies fail, expect degraded status with both flags false
    assert data["status"] == "degraded"
    assert data["ollama"] is False
    assert data["chromadb"] is False


# SCENARIO_ID: 1976a8e5-06d3-4e37-9ba7-ad3249ab7332
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.configuration
def test_health_check_response_includes_correct_llm_model(page: Page):
    """Verify that GET /api/health returns the configured OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL value in the response."""
    page.goto(BASE_URL)
    response = page.context.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200
    data = response.json()
    
    # Response must include llm_model field
    assert "llm_model" in data
    assert isinstance(data["llm_model"], str)
    assert len(data["llm_model"]) > 0
    
    # The llm_model should match the configured value from settings
    expected_model = os.environ.get("OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL", data["llm_model"])
    assert data["llm_model"] == expected_model


# SCENARIO_ID: 20ea7a25-44f8-48aa-8e4f-f38e7e32c07c
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.security
@pytest.mark.public_endpoint
def test_health_check_endpoint_accessible_without_authentication(page: Page):
    """Verify that the health endpoint is intentionally public and does not require authentication headers."""
    page.goto(BASE_URL)
    
    # Make request without any Authorization header
    response = page.context.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/health")
    
    # Should return 200 OK, not 401 Unauthorized
    assert response.status == 200
    data = response.json()
    
    # Verify response is valid health payload
    assert "status" in data
    assert "ollama" in data
    assert "chromadb" in data
    assert "llm_model" in data
    
    # Status should be one of the valid values
    assert data["status"] in ["ok", "degraded"]


# SCENARIO_ID: 99084b03-9f09-46fa-b00a-57593b95d9a4
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.concurrency
def test_health_check_handles_concurrent_requests_without_race_conditions(page: Page):
    """Verify that multiple simultaneous requests to GET /api/health do not cause race conditions."""
    page.goto(BASE_URL)
    
    num_requests = 10
    results = []
    
    def make_health_request(_):
        try:
            resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/health", timeout=10)
            return {"status": resp.status_code, "data": resp.json()}
        except Exception as e:
            return {"error": str(e)}
    
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=num_requests) as executor:
        futures = [executor.submit(make_health_request, i) for i in range(num_requests)]
        for future in as_completed(futures):
            results.append(future.result())
    
    # All requests should have succeeded
    assert len(results) == num_requests
    
    # All requests should return 200 OK
    for i, result in enumerate(results):
        assert "error" not in result, f"Request {i} failed with error: {result.get('error')}"
        assert result["status"] == 200, f"Request {i} returned status {result['status']}, expected 200"
        
        # Verify consistent health data structure
        data = result["data"]
        assert "status" in data
        assert "ollama" in data
        assert "chromadb" in data
        assert "llm_model" in data
        assert data["status"] in ["ok", "degraded"]
    
    # All responses should have consistent data
    first_status = results[0]["data"]["status"]
    for result in results[1:]:
        assert result["data"]["status"] == first_status, "Inconsistent status across concurrent requests"


# SCENARIO_ID: 26cd90f0-e5fb-47af-a4d0-851dd9f34725
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.security
@pytest.mark.information_disclosure
def test_health_check_exception_details_not_exposed_in_response(page: Page):
    """Verify that exception stack traces are logged internally but not returned in the API response."""
    page.goto(BASE_URL)
    response = page.context.request.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200
    data = response.json()
    
    # Response should only contain expected fields
    expected_keys = {"status", "ollama", "chromadb", "llm_model"}
    actual_keys = set(data.keys())
    
    # No extra fields should be present
    assert actual_keys == expected_keys, f"Unexpected fields in response: {actual_keys - expected_keys}"
    
    # Convert to string and check for common exception indicators
    response_str = str(data).lower()
    exception_indicators = [
        "traceback",
        "exception",
        "error:",
        "stack trace",
        "stacktrace",
        "file \"",
        "line ",
        "in ",
        "raise ",
        "<class ",
        "attributes:",
        "details:",
    ]
    
    for indicator in exception_indicators:
        assert indicator not in response_str, f"Response contains potential exception detail: '{indicator}'"
    
    # Verify values are of expected types (no exception objects)
    assert isinstance(data["status"], str)
    assert isinstance(data["ollama"], bool)
    assert isinstance(data["chromadb"], bool)
    assert isinstance(data["llm_model"], str)
